order.label: Tuner
order.reason: Place your tuner first to get the cleanest, most accurate signal for tuning.

order.label: Wah / Filter
order.reason: Wah pedals respond best to a clean signal before any gain stages.

order.label: Compressor
order.reason: Compressor evens out your dynamics before hitting gain pedals.

order.label: Overdrive
order.reason: Light gain first. Overdrive stacks beautifully into distortion.

order.label: Distortion
order.reason: Main gain stage. Place after overdrive for stacking.

order.label: Fuzz
order.reason: Fuzz can go before or after other dirt — experiment with placement.

order.label: EQ
order.reason: After gain stages to shape your distorted tone.

order.label: Phaser
order.reason: Modulation effects after dirt for a cleaner sweep.

order.label: Chorus
order.reason: Chorus after dirt adds lush width to your tone.

order.label: Tremolo
order.reason: Volume-based effect works best after gain and modulation.

order.label: Delay
order.reason: Time-based effects near the end so repeats stay clean.

order.label: Reverb
order.reason: Reverb last (before looper) for natural-sounding space.

order.label: Looper
order.reason: Very last to capture your complete signal with all effects.

tips: There are no absolute rules — these are guidelines. Trust your ears.
Try your fuzz before the wah for a different, more aggressive sound.
Volume pedals can go in multiple positions depending on your needs.
If using an effects loop, place time-based effects (delay, reverb) in the loop.
